description: UAT test scenarios. Each test applies its `setup` op list to a fresh sandbox session, then the user plays through the `instructions` and reports Pass/Fail/Skip + an optional comment. Results append to data/tests/results.jsonl on the server.

ops.reset: Reset sandbox state (empty board, empty hands, fresh player stats).
ops.set_player: {player_idx, field, value} — set a player field (current_mana, max_mana, hp).
ops.add_to_hand: {player_idx, card_id} — add a card to the player's hand.
ops.place: {player_idx, card_id, row, col, [current_health], [dark_matter]} — deploy a minion directly onto the board. Optional current_health pre-damages the minion (min 1).

tests.title: 14.7-09: 3-deep react chain — full conveyor + LIFO resolution
tests.instructions:
GOAL: walk a 3-card react chain end-to-end so you can see the push direction (each new card glides RIGHT→LEFT, the previous one slides off-screen-LEFT) and the LIFO resolution after 👍 (each top card glides LEFT→RIGHT while the one below it returns from off-screen-LEFT to LEFT, until the original Acidic Rain resolves last and fades).

Setup state: P1 has Acidic Rain (5 mana) + Prohibition (4 mana) + 9 mana. P2 has Prohibition (4 mana) + 4 mana. P2 has a Red Diodebot on the board for the burn outcome check.

1. Confirm Controlling = P1. Click Acidic Rain in P1's bottom hand, click again to confirm. Acidic Rain flies into the RIGHT slot then glides over to LEFT, with the placeholder on RIGHT.
2. Toggle Controlling → P2 (right sidebar). Click Prohibition in P2's TOP hand, click again to confirm. Prohibition lands on RIGHT, then the conveyor pushes Acidic Rain off-screen-LEFT and Prohibition glides into LEFT. Placeholder returns on RIGHT.
3. Toggle Controlling → P1. Click Prohibition in P1's bottom hand, click again to confirm. Same conveyor — the previous Prohibition slides off-screen-LEFT, P1's Prohibition lands in LEFT.
4. Toggle Controlling → P2. P2 has no react left so the window auto-drains.
5. Watch the resolution sequence in the centre.
tests.expected:
After the auto-drain (~0.5s), 👍 appears in the RIGHT slot. Then the LIFO resolution plays:
- Beat 1: P1's Prohibition glides LEFT→RIGHT, P2's Prohibition returns from off-screen-LEFT to LEFT. Brief side-by-side beat showing P1's prohibition negating P2's prohibition. P1's prohibition fades.
- Beat 2: P2's Prohibition glides LEFT→RIGHT, Acidic Rain returns from off-screen-LEFT to LEFT. Side-by-side beat. P2's Prohibition fades.
- Beat 3: Acidic Rain alone in LEFT — no card behind it to fill, so no glide. Just holds with 👍 on RIGHT, then fades in place.
- Whole overlay fades out, then the TURN banner blooms (deferred). The Red Diodebot is BURNING (🔥 badge) — P2's Prohibition was negated by P1's, so Acidic Rain resolved.
- Final card destinations: Acidic Rain + P1's Prohibition in P1's grave; P2's Prohibition in P2's grave.

tests.title: 14.7-09: TURN X / PLAYER X banner stacks on turn flip
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the centred turn banner when the turn flips from P1 to P2.

1. Click the Common Rat in your bottom hand.
2. Click any green-tinted tile in the bottom two rows of the board to deploy it.
3. Wait ~1 second. The opponent has no react cards, so the react window auto-closes and the turn flips to P2.
tests.expected: A banner blooms in the centre of the screen with two STACKED lines — 'TURN 2' on top and 'PLAYER 2' directly beneath it (NOT side-by-side). It scales up, holds ~1s, then fades out within ~2s. While visible the banner does not block clicks on the UI behind it.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Fallen Paladin End: heal blip fires on turn end
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the end-of-turn trigger animation (source-tile pulse + ⏳ glyph at centre) and the resulting +2 self-heal on the Paladin.

Setup state: P1 has a damaged Fallen Paladin on the board (current 30/42 🤍, visible in your bottom area) and a Common Rat in hand with 1 mana. P1 is the active player.

1. Click the Common Rat in your bottom hand. Click any green-tinted tile in the bottom two rows to deploy it. (This consumes P1's action and ends the turn.)
2. The react window auto-closes (P2 has no react cards). Watch the Fallen Paladin's tile and the centre of the screen as P1's End-of-Turn fires (just before the turn flips to P2).
tests.expected: Before the turn flips: the Fallen Paladin's tile briefly pulses yellow and an ⏳ glyph blooms in the centre of the screen, then the Paladin's HP ticks up from 30 to 32 🤍 (End: passive_heal +2 to itself). The TURN 2 / PLAYER 2 banner blooms only after the react window has fully closed. No console errors.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Emberplague Rat End: burn-aura blip fires on turn end
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the end-of-turn trigger animation (source pulse → ⏳ glyph → adjacent enemy pulses + becomes burning) when P1's turn ends.

Setup state: P1 has an Emberplague Rat (your bottom area). P2 has a Common Rat one tile in front of it. P1 has a Common Rat in hand and 1 mana — you control P1.

1. Click the Common Rat in your bottom hand. Then click any green-tinted tile to deploy it. (This consumes P1's action and ends the turn.)
2. The react window auto-closes (P2 has no react cards). Watch the centre of the screen and the enemy Common Rat tile as P1's End-of-Turn fires.
tests.expected: Before the turn flips to P2: the Emberplague Rat's tile pulses, a ⏳ glyph blooms at centre, then the enemy Common Rat tile pulses. After the flip the Common Rat shows a 🔥 burn badge. No console errors.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Priority modal opens when 2+ same-player triggers fire
tests.instructions:
GOAL: see the priority-pick modal that appears when two of P1's end-of-turn triggers fire on the same turn end.

Setup state: P1 has TWO damaged Fallen Paladins on the board (each at 30/42 🤍), a Common Rat in hand, and 1 mana. P1 is the active player.

1. Click the Common Rat in your bottom hand. Click any green-tinted tile in the bottom two rows to deploy it. (Ends P1's turn.)
2. The react window auto-closes. As P1's End-of-Turn resolves, a priority-pick modal opens titled 'Pick the order to resolve these effects' showing TWO Fallen Paladin cards.
3. Click one Paladin card. That trigger fires (blip + heal that Paladin from 30 to 32). The remaining trigger has no other choices to compete with, so it auto-resolves and the modal closes.
tests.expected: Modal opens with two full-art Fallen Paladin cards, styled like the tutor modal (cyan border, dark header bar reading 'Pick the order to resolve these effects' and '2 triggers waiting'). Clicking either card fires that Paladin's blip + heal (30→32 🤍). The remaining trigger then auto-resolves (second Paladin also heals 30→32 🤍) and the modal closes cleanly. The TURN 2 / PLAYER 2 banner blooms only after both heals resolve. Both Paladins on the board now show 16/32 🤍.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Single-card react window: 👍 then lateral glide
tests.instructions:
GOAL: confirm the react-window resolution for a 1-card chain — 👍 appears, then the card glides from LEFT slot laterally into the RIGHT slot (replacing the 👍), then fades.

Setup state: P1 has Acidic Rain (5 mana) in hand and 5 mana available. P2 has a Red Diodebot on the board.

1. Click Acidic Rain in P1's bottom hand (confirm-armed glow appears).
2. Click Acidic Rain a SECOND time to confirm the cast.
3. The react window appears in the centre: Acidic Rain flies into the RIGHT slot, then glides over to the LEFT slot with a placeholder on the RIGHT. The opponent has no react cards so the window auto-drains.
4. Watch the resolution.
tests.expected: After the auto-drain (~0.5s): a green 👍 appears in the RIGHT slot. About half a second later, the Acidic Rain card glides laterally from the LEFT slot to the RIGHT slot (replacing the 👍 — it does NOT fly off-screen). It then fades in place and the whole overlay fades out. Red Diodebot on the board becomes burning (🔥 badge). The card never slides off to the right edge.

tests.title: 14.7-09: Two-card react chain resolves backwards with off-screen return
tests.instructions:
GOAL: confirm the multi-card resolution flow — the top of the stack glides LEFT→RIGHT while the card below it returns from off-screen-LEFT to the LEFT slot, showing 'right card resolves against left card'.

Setup state: P1 has Acidic Rain (5 mana) and 5 mana. P2 has Prohibition (4 mana, react: negates an opponent's magic) and 4 mana, plus a Red Diodebot on the board.

1. Confirm Controlling = P1. Click Acidic Rain in P1's bottom hand, then click it again to confirm the cast.
2. Acidic Rain enters the RIGHT slot and glides over to the LEFT, with a placeholder on the RIGHT. The window is open, waiting for P2's react.
3. Toggle Controlling → P2 (right sidebar). Click Prohibition in P2's TOP hand, click again to confirm.
4. Prohibition enters the RIGHT slot, then the conveyor pushes: Acidic Rain slides off-screen to the LEFT and Prohibition glides over to the LEFT slot. RIGHT shows the placeholder again.
5. Toggle Controlling → P1. P1 has no react, so the window auto-drains.
6. Watch the resolution sequence in the centre.
tests.expected:
After the auto-drain (~0.5s): a green 👍 appears in the RIGHT slot, marking the chain finished. Then:
- Beat 1 (~0.5s in): Prohibition glides laterally from LEFT to RIGHT (replacing the 👍). At the SAME TIME, Acidic Rain returns from off-screen-LEFT into the LEFT slot. You briefly see Prohibition (right) and Acidic Rain (left) side-by-side — Prohibition is resolving against Acidic Rain.
- Beat 2: Prohibition fades.
- Beat 3: Acidic Rain glides laterally LEFT→RIGHT (alone now, no card returns from off-screen). Then fades.
- The whole overlay fades out within ~3s of the auto-drain. Red Diodebot is NOT burning (Prohibition negated the spell). Acidic Rain ends up in P1's grave; Prohibition ends up in P2's grave.

tests.title: React chain — Prohibition vs Prohibition, 3 deep
tests.instructions:
Both players have plenty of mana and three Prohibitions each. You'll drive both sides via the 'Controlling' toggle in the sandbox sidebar.

1. Controlling P1, click Acidic Rain in P1's hand and confirm the cast (2nd click).
2. The spell stage shows [Acidic Rain] ▶ [?]. Toggle Controlling → P2.
3. Click Prohibition in P2's hand. The stage should push Acidic Rain LEFT off-screen and slide Prohibition into the centre with a fresh [?] slot.
4. Toggle Controlling → P1. Click Prohibition in P1's hand. Another slide.
5. Toggle Controlling → P2. Click Prohibition in P2's hand. Another slide.
6. Toggle Controlling → P1. No Prohibition left — click the Skip/Pass button in the react window to close the stack.
tests.expected: The spell stage animates a slide-left / slide-in-right each time a react is added. The '?' resets between reacts. Once the stack resolves (LIFO): the topmost Prohibition negates the second, the second negates the first, the first negates Acidic Rain. No minion ends up burning, all 4 cards (Acidic Rain + 3 Prohibitions) land in the Grave piles (Acidic Rain + P1's 2 Prohibitions in P1 grave, P2's 1 Prohibition in P2 grave). No HP damage popup — the spell was negated.

tests.title: Sacrifice — jumper leaps, shatters mid-arc (default visual)
tests.instructions:
1. P1's Common Rat is pre-placed on row 4 — ready to jump off.
2. Click the Rat to select it.
3. Click the Sacrifice action button.
tests.expected: Beat 1: white-purple flash erupts on the tile. Beat 2: the pixel-art jumper silhouette (purple outline, white glow) leaps forward toward P2's HP. Beat 3: mid-arc the silhouette explodes into 8 hexagonal purple shards that fly outward spinning and fade. HP damage popup fires after. A+C combined — this is the default.

tests.title: Acidic Rain burns all Metal/Robot/Machine minions — and nothing else
tests.instructions:
1. Click Acidic Rain in P1's hand.
2. Click any valid play indicator to cast it (it's 5 mana; P1 has 20).
tests.expected: All three Diodebots, the RGB Lasercannon, and the Sparkbot on P2's side should light up as BURNING (look for the fire glow/border). The Common Rat on P2 must NOT be burning.

tests.title: Live Dark Matter count in card text
tests.instructions:
1. Hover (or click) Dark Matter Barrage in P1's hand.
2. Read the rules line.
tests.expected: The line should read 'Deal 5 + 9 (Dark Matter) damage to all enemies' (approximately) with the number 9 glowing in PURPLE. Each of P1's three Mages has 3 Dark Matter stacks, summing to 9.

tests.title: Magic cast animation — card flies from hand to grave
tests.instructions:
1. Play Dark Matter Stash from P1's hand (5 mana).
2. Watch the card as it leaves the hand.
tests.expected: The Dark Matter Stash card should visibly fly from its hand slot upward and land on the P1 Grave pile (bottom-right pile buttons), shrinking and fading as it goes. It should NOT just disappear instantly.

tests.title: Spell cast center stage — magic card appears in centre with ▶ ? slot
tests.instructions:
1. Play Dark Matter Stash from P1's hand (5 mana).
2. Watch the center of the screen immediately after.
tests.expected: A giant Dark Matter Stash appears in the middle of the screen with a cyan ▶ arrow and a '?' slot to its right. After roughly 1 second the '?' flips to a green 👍 (thumbs-up) and the whole overlay fades out.

tests.title: Feed the Shadow — magic spell with destroy-ally cost actually casts
tests.instructions:
1. P1 has 2 mana, a Common Rat at (3,2), and Feed the Shadow in hand.
2. Click Feed the Shadow (with only one legal target the cast auto-resolves).
tests.expected: The card casts without an 'Illegal action' error. Your Common Rat at (3,2) is destroyed. The enemy Rat takes damage equal to the destroyed Rat's attack (10). Your Gargoyle Sorceress (Dark Mage) on the board gains +1 Dark Matter.

tests.title: Gargoyle Sorceress — player picks BOTH discard cards
tests.instructions:
1. Click Gargoyle Sorceress in P1's hand.
2. Pick any empty back-two-row tile to deploy.
3. The picker modal should appear showing ALL 3 other hand cards.
4. Click the first card you want to exhaust. Modal stays open; the card becomes dimmed with a ✓.
5. Click a second card. As soon as you hit the required count (2), Gargoyle deploys and both cards go to the Exhaust pile.
tests.expected: The picker modal does NOT submit after just one click. It waits for 2 picks (progress shows '1 / 2 picked' then '2 / 2 picked'). Clicking an already-picked card un-picks it. After the second pick the Gargoyle Sorceress appears on the chosen tile and exactly the 2 cards you picked land in P1's Exhaust pile.

tests.title: All pickers share the same visual style (tutor is the reference)
tests.instructions:
1. Play 'To The Ratmobile!' from P1's hand (3 mana; tutors 2 Rats from deck). Observe the tutor modal — note its chrome (cyan border, dark header bar with title, card grid, close button).
2. Click Reset in the test panel.
3. Play Gargoyle Sorceress from P1's hand (5 mana) — it needs to discard 2 cards. Observe the discard picker modal.
tests.expected: The discard picker modal uses the SAME visual chrome as the tutor modal — cyan border, dark header bar with title text, card grid body, consistent spacing. The words 'sacrifice' should NOT appear anywhere; this is a DISCARD (cards go to Exhaust). Title should read 'Pick 2 cards to Discard'.

tests.title: Matter Possessed — Cost: Deal 25🤍 to own face
tests.instructions:
1. Hover Matter Possessed in P1's hand and confirm the cost line reads 'Cost: Deal 25🤍 to own face'.
2. P1 HP is 30 and mana is 1.
3. Play Matter Possessed onto any empty tile in P1's back two rows.
tests.expected: P1's HP drops from 30 to 5 (dealt 25 to face). A 25/25 Dark Mage minion appears on the chosen tile. Mana drops from 1 to 0.

tests.title: 14.8-04c: Ratchanter activated ability — tutor → conjure_deploy modal chain
tests.instructions:
GOAL: verify the Ratchanter 'Conjure Common Rat' activated ability drives two sequential pending modals (pending_tutor → conjure_deploy) through the unified eventQueue without visual glitches.

Setup state: P1 has a Ratchanter on row 1 col 2 and 3 mana (enough to cover the 2-mana activation). P1's deck contains 3 Common Rats (the tutor pool). P1 also has one existing Common Rat on row 2 col 1 so the buff step has a target. P1 is active.

1. Click the Ratchanter minion tile to select it. The activated ability button ('Conjure Common Rat — 2 mana') should appear on the minion panel or context menu.
2. Click the activate button. Expected sequence: the buff fires first (existing Rat on row 2 col 1 gets +1🗡️/+1🤍 — popup '+1/+1' or similar on its tile). Then a pending_tutor modal opens showing the 3 Rats from the deck — pick one.
3. Pick the first Rat card in the tutor modal. The tutor modal closes; a conjure_deploy tile picker opens (green-highlighted friendly-zone tiles).
4. Click any green friendly-zone tile. A new Common Rat appears there; mana drops to 1; turn stays with P1 (activated ability doesn't consume the action).
tests.expected: The two modals fire SEQUENTIALLY, each gating the event queue until resolved: tutor opens first; after the pick the tutor closes + conjure_deploy picker opens immediately; after the tile pick the Rat is deployed. No visual beats overlap — the buff popup on the existing Rat finishes BEFORE the tutor modal opens; the tutor closes cleanly before the deploy-tile picker appears; the new Rat summon animation plays AFTER the deploy tile click. The eventQueue scrim (very faint fixed overlay) may be visible while modals are open but does not block modal clicks. No console errors. IF this scenario appears to bypass the modal (Rat appears instantly without tile-picker) that is the engine-unwired conjure_deploy issue documented in 04b SUMMARY — mark it N/A and note the engine still emits pending_* state fields but not EVT_PENDING_MODAL_OPENED.

tests.title: 14.8-04c: Tree Wyrm react on opponent end-of-turn — react window → tutor chain
tests.instructions:
GOAL: verify the multi-purpose Tree Wyrm react (opponent_ends_turn condition, 1-mana discard for tutor) plays cleanly through the eventQueue — BEFORE_END_OF_TURN react window opens, P1 plays Tree Wyrm from hand as a react, spell stage shows it, tutor modal opens for up to 2 Tree Wyrms from deck, all beats discrete.

Setup state: P1 has a Tree Wyrm in hand + 1 mana. P1's deck contains 2 additional Tree Wyrms (tutor targets). P2 is active and has just the auto-drawn card in hand (nothing to play — will immediately PASS).

1. Confirm Controlling = P2 (right sidebar). Click the PASS button in P2's controls (or if PASS requires an action first, toggle via the sidebar cheat). This emits P2's turn-end signal — the engine opens BEFORE_END_OF_TURN react window targeted at P1.
2. Toggle Controlling → P1. P1's hand should show Tree Wyrm highlighted as a legal react (cyan/teal border on react-face hover).
3. Click Tree Wyrm in P1's hand. The react-confirm prompt appears. Click again to confirm.
4. Spell stage opens showing Tree Wyrm (react face). P2 has no counter-react so the window auto-drains.
5. React resolves: tutor modal opens offering up to 2 Tree Wyrms from P1's deck. Pick 1 or 2. Close the picker (or pick until exhausted).
6. Turn flips to P1 (since P2's turn ended and their react window is now drained). TURN 3 / PLAYER 1 banner blooms.
tests.expected: All beats discrete in order: (a) P2 PASS → BEFORE_END_OF_TURN react window opens, (b) Tree Wyrm react-arm prompt → confirm, (c) Tree Wyrm animates into spell stage LEFT slot, (d) window auto-drains with 👍, (e) tutor modal opens for Tree Wyrm picks, (f) picks close the modal, (g) Tree Wyrm from P1's hand goes to P1's graveyard + picked Tree Wyrms go to P1's hand, (h) spell stage fades out, (i) turn flips to P1 with TURN banner, (j) no console errors. IF Tree Wyrm does not appear as a legal react on P2's end-of-turn (engine-unwired for opponent_ends_turn react_condition on the multi-purpose minion card type): mark N/A and note the card_loader behavior. Alternative fallback: play Tree Wyrm as a normal 5-mana minion to verify the Summon: Draw trigger + spell_stage + minion_summoned + card_drawn event sequence plays cleanly — also covers eventQueue pipeline for the minion face.
